betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Data Storage: Disk Drives & HDDs§f

§aDeploy non-volatile storage to save files, scripts, and execute operating systems!§f
Computers have zero persistent storage built-in. Without a disk drive or hard drive, any files you write will be permanently lost when the computer restarts or runs out of power.

§6Storage Options§f:
§b• Disk Drive§f: A block that reads and writes §bFloppy Disks§f.
§b• Floppy Disks§f: Portable storage mediums that hold up to §e512 KB§f of data. Essential for installing operating systems.
§b• Hard Disk Drives (HDDs)§f: Internal storage components that fit directly into case expansion slots:
  §e• Tier 1 HDD (Green)§f: Stores up to §e1 MB§f of data.
  §e• Tier 2 HDD (Yellow)§f: Stores up to §e2 MB§f of data.
  §e• Tier 3 HDD (Cyan)§f: Stores up to §e4 MB§f of data.

§6Booting OpenOS§f:
1. Insert a §bTier 1/2/3 HDD§f and a §bDisk Drive§f into your computer case.
2. Place the §bOpenOS Floppy Disk§f into the disk drive.
3. Turn on the computer. It will boot into the temporary floppy environment.
4. Type §9install§f in the terminal to copy OpenOS permanently to your internal HDD. Once installed, remove the floppy disk!

§cEnergy Cost of Filesystems§f:
Reading and writing data requires power:
§b• Read Cost§f: §e0.1 energy per kilobyte§f read.
§b• Write Cost§f: §e0.25 energy per kilobyte§f written.
§eNote§f: Non-I/O operations (such as listing files with §9ls§f or running §9df§f to check free space) do not consume any power.
